goal: Sync Stripe payments to Xero accounting
description: When a Stripe payment succeeds, create a matching invoice and payment record in Xero for automated bookkeeping.

steps.action: Retrieve completed payment intent with customer and line item details
steps.error_hint: Amount is in cents (integer). Divide by 100 for Xero. Currency must be uppercase ISO 4217.

steps.action: Create or match contact in Xero by email, then create invoice and apply payment
steps.error_hint: Xero-tenant-id header is REQUIRED on every call. Amount must be decimal (not cents). Check fiscal period is open.

steps.action: Post payment sync confirmation to #accounting channel
steps.error_hint: Slack returns 200 OK even on failure — check ok:true in response body.

gotchas: Stripe amounts are cents (integer), Xero amounts are decimal — always divide by 100
Xero requires Xero-tenant-id header on every request — get it during OAuth flow
Multi-currency: Stripe and Xero must agree on currency code or reconciliation fails silently

goal: Stream Adyen payment data to BigQuery for analytics
description: Capture Adyen payment events and load them into BigQuery for dashboarding and fraud analysis.

steps.action: Fetch recent payment results from Adyen with payment method and risk scores
steps.error_hint: Amounts in minor units: {value:10000, currency:'USD'} = $100. Test URL vs live URL differ — check environment.

steps.action: Stream insert payment records into BigQuery payments table
steps.error_hint: Set useLegacySql:false. Streaming inserts have ~90 second delay before queryable. Schema must pre-exist.

steps.action: Post daily payment summary to #payments channel
steps.error_hint: Format amounts back to decimal for human readability in Slack message.

gotchas: Adyen minor units vary by currency (JPY has no decimals, EUR has 2) — use currency exponent table
BigQuery streaming insert buffer means data is not immediately queryable — don't chain a query right after insert

goal: Build RAG knowledge base from web content
description: Crawl target websites with Firecrawl, generate embeddings, and upsert into Pinecone for retrieval-augmented generation.

steps.action: Crawl target URL and return clean markdown pages
steps.error_hint: Crawl is async — returns job_id. Poll GET /crawl/{id} until status='completed'. Each page costs 1 credit.

steps.action: Generate embeddings for each crawled page using text-embedding-3-small
steps.error_hint: Max 8191 tokens per input. Split long pages into chunks of ~500 tokens with overlap. Model outputs 1536 dimensions.

steps.action: Upsert vectors with page content as metadata into Pinecone index
steps.error_hint: Index must be created with dimension=1536 matching embedding model. Use namespace for source separation. Api-Key header (not Bearer).

gotchas: Pinecone dimension MUST match embedding model: 1536 for text-embedding-3-small, 3072 for large
Firecrawl crawl is async — build a polling loop, don't assume immediate completion
Chunk strategy matters: 500 tokens with 50-token overlap is a good default for RAG

goal: Local RAG development with Firecrawl and Chroma
description: Scrape documentation sites and store in local Chroma for development-time RAG without cloud dependencies.

steps.action: Scrape target documentation pages to clean markdown
steps.error_hint: Use /scrape for single pages (sync), /crawl for full sites (async). formats=['markdown'] for LLM-ready output.

steps.action: Add documents to Chroma collection with auto-embedding
steps.error_hint: v2 API path: /tenants/{tenant}/databases/{db}/collections. NOT the old v1 /collections path. If embedding function configured, pass documents directly.

gotchas: Chroma v2 changed collection routing — use /tenants/default_tenant/databases/default_database/collections
Local Chroma runs with no auth by default — fine for dev but set CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_PROVIDER for production

goal: AI-powered competitive research pipeline
description: Use Tavily for broad search, Perplexity for deep analysis, and store findings in Notion.

steps.action: Search for recent information on target topic with AI-optimized results
steps.error_hint: Use search_depth='advanced' for deeper results (costs more credits). api_key goes in POST body, not header.

steps.action: Deep-dive analysis using Tavily findings as context, with web citations
steps.error_hint: Use model='sonar-pro' for best quality with citations. API requires payment method even for first use.

steps.action: Create research page in Notion database with analysis and sources
steps.error_hint: Notion API uses block-based content — convert markdown to Notion blocks. Max 100 blocks per request.

gotchas: Tavily api_key goes in POST body (not Authorization header) — non-standard
Perplexity citations array may contain URLs that are no longer accessible — cache key findings

goal: Automate hire-to-onboard pipeline
description: When a candidate is marked as hired in Greenhouse ATS, create their employee record in BambooHR and notify HR on Slack.

steps.action: Retrieve hired candidate details from Greenhouse Harvest API
steps.error_hint: Basic auth: API key as username, empty string as password. Per-page max 500 for bulk queries.

steps.action: Create employee record in BambooHR with hire details
steps.error_hint: URL requires subdomain (company-specific). Basic auth: API key as username, literal 'x' as password. Employee ID is in Location header, not body.

steps.action: Notify #hr-onboarding channel with new hire details
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true in response. Use blocks for rich formatting.

gotchas: Both Greenhouse and BambooHR use Basic auth but with different quirks: Greenhouse = key:'' (empty password), BambooHR = key:'x' (literal x)
BambooHR returns employee ID in Location header, not response body — parse the header
Employee fields are configurable per BambooHR account — use GET /meta/fields to check available fields first

goal: Convert support tickets to engineering tasks
description: When a Freshdesk ticket is tagged as a bug or feature request, create a corresponding ClickUp task and link back.

steps.action: Retrieve ticket details including description, priority, and customer info
steps.error_hint: Auth is API key with literal 'X' as password in Basic auth (same pattern as BambooHR). Priority is integer: 1=Low, 4=Urgent.

steps.action: Create task in engineering list with ticket context
steps.error_hint: Authorization: {api_key} — NO 'Bearer' prefix. Priority mapping differs: ClickUp uses 1=Urgent, 4=Low (reversed from Freshdesk).

steps.action: Add note to original ticket with ClickUp task link
steps.error_hint: Use private note (private:true) so customer doesn't see internal engineering link.

gotchas: Priority scales are REVERSED: Freshdesk 1=Low/4=Urgent, ClickUp 1=Urgent/4=Low — build a mapping
ClickUp Authorization header takes raw key without Bearer prefix — common 401 trap

goal: Enrich support tickets with order data
description: When a Gorgias ticket arrives, look up the customer's recent orders in BigCommerce and attach context to the ticket.

steps.action: Retrieve ticket and customer email from Gorgias
steps.error_hint: Basic auth: email as username, API key as password. Subdomain is account-specific.

steps.action: Search orders by customer email in BigCommerce
steps.error_hint: Two headers required: X-Auth-Token AND X-Auth-Client. Orders use v2 API path (/v2/orders), not v3.

steps.action: Add internal note with order summary to the ticket
steps.error_hint: Message type must be 'internal-note' to keep order details private from customer.

gotchas: BigCommerce orders endpoint is v2, not v3 — different base path than catalog endpoints
BigCommerce requires BOTH X-Auth-Token and X-Auth-Client headers — missing either gives 401

goal: CI/CD pipeline to Fly.io edge
description: On GitHub push, run tests via GitHub Actions, then deploy to Fly.io global edge network.

steps.action: Detect push event and trigger Actions workflow
steps.error_hint: Use fine-grained PAT with actions:write scope. Check .github/workflows/ exists.

steps.action: Wait for CI workflow to complete and check status
steps.error_hint: Poll GET /actions/runs/{id} — status goes queued→in_progress→completed. conclusion='success' means ready to deploy.

steps.action: Deploy to Fly.io using flyctl machine API if CI passed
steps.error_hint: Fly Machines API uses Bearer token from FLY_API_TOKEN. Base URL is api.machines.dev. Ensure app exists first.

gotchas: GitHub Actions workflow must complete before deploy — poll status, don't fire-and-forget
Fly.io Machines API is at api.machines.dev (not fly.io/api) — different domain

goal: CI/CD with CircleCI tests and Render deploy
description: GitHub push triggers CircleCI pipeline for testing, then deploys to Render on success.

steps.action: Detect push to main branch
steps.error_hint: Filter for branch=main to avoid deploying feature branches.

steps.action: Trigger pipeline and wait for workflow completion
steps.error_hint: CircleCI API v2 uses project slug format: gh/{org}/{repo}. Personal API token as Bearer.

steps.action: Trigger deploy on Render service
steps.error_hint: Render API key as Bearer token. Deploy is async — poll GET /services/{id}/deploys/{deployId} for completion.

gotchas: CircleCI project slug format is specific: gh/{org}/{repo} — not just the repo name
Render deploy is async — poll for completion before declaring success

goal: Sync contractor payments to accounting
description: When Deel processes a contractor payment, record the expense in Xero with proper categorization.

steps.action: Retrieve recent contractor invoices and payment details
steps.error_hint: Deel handles both EOR employees and contractors — filter by contract_type. Currency is per-contract (multi-currency).

steps.action: Create bill (accounts payable) entries in Xero for each contractor payment
steps.error_hint: Use Type='ACCPAY' for bills (not ACCREC which is receivables). Xero-tenant-id header required on every call.

steps.action: Post contractor payment summary to #finance channel
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true in Slack response.

gotchas: Deel is multi-currency by design — Xero bill currency must match the contract currency, not your base currency
Xero bill type is 'ACCPAY' (accounts payable), NOT 'ACCREC' (accounts receivable) — wrong type silently creates wrong entries

goal: Abandoned cart recovery via email automation
description: Detect abandoned carts in BigCommerce and trigger recovery email sequences in ActiveCampaign.

steps.action: Fetch abandoned carts older than 1 hour with customer details
steps.error_hint: Two headers: X-Auth-Token + X-Auth-Client. Abandoned carts are in v3: /carts?include=line_items.

steps.action: Create or update contacts and add to abandoned cart automation
steps.error_hint: Auth header is 'Api-Token' — NOT Authorization Bearer. Base URL is {account}.api-us1.com. Use POST /contact/sync for upsert.

gotchas: ActiveCampaign auth header is 'Api-Token' (not Bearer) and base URL contains account name — double whammy for generic HTTP clients
BigCommerce cart API and ActiveCampaign contacts must be de-duped by email to avoid duplicate recovery emails

goal: Form submission to nurture sequence
description: Typeform form completion triggers a behavioral nurture sequence in Customer.io.

steps.action: Retrieve form response with answers keyed by field ref
steps.error_hint: Responses are keyed by field.ref — set stable refs during form creation. Personal access token as Bearer.

steps.action: Create or update customer and track form_completed event
steps.error_hint: Track API uses Basic auth (site_id:api_key), NOT Bearer. Customer ID is YOUR choice — use email or internal ID.

gotchas: Customer.io has TWO APIs with DIFFERENT auth: Track API = Basic auth (site_id:api_key), App API = Bearer token
Typeform responses without stable field refs will break when form is edited — always set refs at form creation

goal: Add AI voice narration to CMS content
description: Generate audio narration with ElevenLabs for Webflow CMS blog posts, storing audio URLs in CMS items.

steps.action: Fetch blog post content from Webflow CMS collection
steps.error_hint: v2 API is current (v1 deprecated). Field names are slugified versions of CMS field labels.

steps.action: Convert blog text to speech audio using multilingual model
steps.error_hint: Auth header is xi-api-key (NOT Bearer). Response is binary audio (mpeg) — save to file or upload to CDN. Use eleven_multilingual_v2 for non-English.

steps.action: Update CMS item with audio URL and publish
steps.error_hint: After CMS update, must call POST /sites/{id}/publish to make changes live.

gotchas: ElevenLabs returns binary audio — you need a CDN or storage service to host it before linking in Webflow
ElevenLabs auth is xi-api-key header (not Bearer) — common 401 trap
Webflow CMS changes require explicit publish call — updates are staged, not live

goal: Self-hosted embedding pipeline with open models
description: Use HuggingFace Inference API for embeddings and store in Qdrant for open-source RAG without OpenAI dependency.

steps.action: Generate embeddings using open-source model (e.g. BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5)
steps.error_hint: Inference API uses Bearer token. Model must be deployed or use serverless inference. Embedding dimension varies by model (1024 for bge-large).

steps.action: Upsert vectors with text metadata into Qdrant collection
steps.error_hint: Collection vector size MUST match model dimension (1024 for bge-large, not 1536). Use Cosine distance for normalized embeddings. Api-Key header for Qdrant Cloud.

gotchas: Vector dimension MUST match between HuggingFace model and Qdrant collection — bge-large=1024, not OpenAI's 1536
HuggingFace serverless inference has cold starts (~10-30s) — first request may timeout
Qdrant gRPC is 5x faster than REST for bulk upserts — use it for large batches

goal: Reranked semantic search for better RAG
description: Query Pinecone for candidate results, then use Cohere Rerank to improve relevance ordering.

steps.action: Query Pinecone index with embedding for top-k candidates
steps.error_hint: Query top_k should be 3-5x your final desired results (reranking filters). Api-Key header, not Bearer.

steps.action: Rerank candidate texts against the original query for improved relevance
steps.error_hint: Use model='rerank-v3.5'. Input documents are strings, not embeddings. Bearer token auth.

gotchas: Fetch 3-5x more candidates from Pinecone than you need — reranking works best with a larger candidate pool
Cohere rerank input is TEXT (not vectors) — pass the original document text from Pinecone metadata
Cohere Embed v3 uses input_type='search_document' for indexing and 'search_query' for queries — mixing them degrades quality

goal: Ultra-fast LLM responses with Redis cache
description: Use Groq's fast inference for LLM calls and cache results in Redis to avoid redundant API calls.

steps.action: Check Redis cache for existing response to this query hash
steps.error_hint: NEVER use KEYS command in production (O(n) blocks server). Use GET with a deterministic hash key. Set TTL on all keys.

steps.action: If cache miss, call Groq for fast LLM inference
steps.error_hint: OpenAI SDK compatible — just set base_url to https://api.groq.com/openai/v1. Use llama-3.3-70b-versatile for quality.

steps.action: Cache the Groq response with TTL for future lookups
steps.error_hint: Always use SET with EX (TTL). Unbounded key growth is the #1 Redis incident.

gotchas: Redis KEYS command is O(n) and blocks the server — use SCAN or direct GET with hash keys
Groq is OpenAI SDK compatible — just change base_url, no code rewrite needed
Always set TTL on cached LLM responses — stale AI answers are worse than no cache

goal: LLM cost and quality monitoring
description: Monitor Langfuse traces for cost spikes and quality issues, alert on Slack when thresholds are exceeded.

steps.action: Query recent traces for cost and latency metrics
steps.error_hint: Basic auth: public_key as username, secret_key as password. Use /traces?limit=100 with date filters.

steps.action: Post LLM observability alert if cost or latency exceeds threshold
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true. Use blocks with color-coded attachment (red for critical, yellow for warning).

gotchas: Langfuse Basic auth uses public_key:secret_key (not API key:empty) — both values are needed
Langfuse self-hosted has no rate limit but cloud is 1000 req/min — batch trace queries

goal: Route support issues to engineering
description: When a Help Scout conversation is tagged as a bug, create a Linear issue and link back to the conversation.

steps.action: Retrieve conversation with customer context and thread history
steps.error_hint: HAL+JSON format — data is in _embedded. Threads are separate endpoint: /conversations/{id}/threads.

steps.action: Create issue in engineering team with support context
steps.error_hint: Linear uses GraphQL — wrap mutation in { "query": "mutation {...}" }. Personal key: Authorization: {key} (NO Bearer prefix).

steps.action: Add note to Help Scout conversation with Linear issue link
steps.error_hint: OAuth or API key with Basic auth. Use type='note' for internal notes.

gotchas: Linear personal API key: Authorization header takes raw key WITHOUT 'Bearer' prefix — OAuth tokens DO use Bearer
Help Scout uses HAL+JSON — nested resources are in _embedded, not flat in the response

goal: Escalate priority emails from shared inbox
description: When a Front shared inbox conversation is tagged as urgent, immediately notify the relevant Slack channel with context.

steps.action: Retrieve conversation details from Front shared inbox
steps.error_hint: API token as Bearer. Use q[] params for filtering by tag. Plan-based rate limits: Starter 50/min, Growth 100/min.

steps.action: Post urgent escalation to appropriate channel based on conversation tags
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true. Use @mentions to ping the assigned person.

gotchas: Front rate limits vary by plan (50-200 req/min) — check your plan before building high-volume automations
Front conversations unify email/SMS/chat — message body format varies by channel type

goal: Deal-stage triggered multi-channel outreach
description: When a Pipedrive deal moves to negotiation stage, trigger a multi-channel outreach via Brevo (email + SMS).

steps.action: Retrieve deal details with contact and organization info
steps.error_hint: Pipedrive uses api_token as query parameter (not header). Person data is nested — use person_id.email[0].value.

steps.action: Send personalized follow-up email via Brevo transactional API
steps.error_hint: Auth header is lowercase 'api-key' (NOT Authorization: Bearer). POST /smtp/email for transactional.

gotchas: Pipedrive auth is api_token in query string — not a header-based auth
Brevo auth header is lowercase 'api-key' — case matters, it's not standard Bearer
Brevo has separate transactional vs marketing email limits — check which applies to your plan

goal: Reconcile BNPL payments with accounting
description: Match Klarna buy-now-pay-later settlements with Xero for accurate revenue recognition.

steps.action: Fetch settled orders and payout details from Klarna
steps.error_hint: Basic auth (username:password). Amounts in minor units (cents). Sandbox URL is different: api.playground.klarna.com.

steps.action: Create bank transaction in Xero matching Klarna settlement
steps.error_hint: Xero-tenant-id required. Amount must be decimal (divide cents by 100). Use BankTransactionType='RECEIVE' for incoming settlements.

gotchas: Klarna settles NET of fees — the Xero entry must reflect net amount, not gross order value
Klarna amounts are minor units, Xero is decimal — always divide by 100
Klarna sandbox (api.playground.klarna.com) and production (api.klarna.com) are different base URLs

goal: Sync operational data to analytics warehouse
description: Extract data from Neon PostgreSQL and load into BigQuery for analytics dashboarding.

steps.action: Query Neon PostgreSQL for daily aggregated operational data
steps.error_hint: Neon uses standard PostgreSQL connection string. Serverless driver has cold starts (~500ms). Use connection pooling for repeated queries.

steps.action: Load aggregated data into BigQuery analytics table
steps.error_hint: Set useLegacySql:false. Use streaming insert for small batches, load job for large datasets. Free tier: 1TB query/month.

steps.action: Report sync completion with row count
steps.error_hint: Check ok:true.

gotchas: Neon serverless has cold starts — first query after inactivity may take 500ms+
BigQuery streaming inserts are not immediately queryable (~90s buffer) — for dashboards, use load jobs instead

goal: AI sake recommendation for team events
description: Use Sake Navi to recommend sake paired with the planned dinner menu, share recommendation on Slack for team vote.

steps.action: Get food pairing recommendations based on dinner menu
steps.error_hint: No auth required. Uses SSI 4-type classification x 3 pairing principles. Input dishes in Japanese for best results.

steps.action: Post sake recommendations with tasting notes to #team-dinner channel for voting
steps.error_hint: Use blocks with emoji reactions enabled for voting. Check ok:true.

gotchas: Sake Navi input works best in Japanese — English dish names may map to wrong flavor profiles
SSI 4-type classification (薫酒/爽酒/醇酒/熟酒) is the foundation of pairing logic — include type in display

goal: Automated social media scheduling
description: Monitor Twitter engagement metrics and use Buffer to schedule optimized posts across platforms.

steps.action: Fetch recent tweet performance metrics to identify best-performing content
steps.error_hint: Free tier: 1500 tweets read/month. Use tweet.fields=public_metrics for engagement data. OAuth 2.0 PKCE for user context.

steps.action: Schedule posts inspired by top-performing content across connected profiles
steps.error_hint: Request body is form-encoded, NOT JSON. Profile = connected social account. List profiles first (GET /profiles.json).

gotchas: Twitter free tier is extremely limited (1500 reads/month, 50 writes) — cache results aggressively
Buffer request body is form-encoded (NOT JSON) — Content-Type must be application/x-www-form-urlencoded
